preamble: The transformation from a palatalized voiced alveolar plosive (dʲ) to a voiced palatal approximant (j) is a common phonetic shift, representing a process of lenition. This change involves the weakening of the plosive articulation, where the complete closure of the plosive is relaxed to the partial obstruction of an approximant, while the palatalized quality of the original consonant fully manifests as a palatal sound.

examples.note: Example of lenition where dʲ becomes j.
Lenition of dʲ to j, often triggered by specific grammatical contexts.

sources: Doyle, Aidan. 2001. Irish. München: Lincom Europa. (Cited in search result 1)
